name: kgcl-schema
documentation:
# KGCL: Knowledge Graph Change Language

KGCL is a standard datamodel for representing changes in ontologies and knowledge graphs.

This repository houses:

- The KGCL schema/standard
- The Python implementation of the standard (LinkML model, LARK grammar)

The following URIs will resolve to the KGCL standard:

- OWL: https://w3id.org/kgcl/kgcl.owl.ttl
- JSONLD: https://w3id.org/kgcl/kgcl.context.jsonld
- SHACL: https://w3id.org/kgcl/kgcl.shacl.ttl
- YAML: https://w3id.org/kgcl/kgcl.yaml

## Documentation

[Read more here.](https://incatools.github.io/kgcl/)

modules.name: kgcl_schema.utils
documentation:
Convenience wrappers around linkml runtime.

Needed for doing basic conversion between objects and serialization formats.
The top level class is a Session object
Some of this will become unnecessary in the future

In particular, until linkml supports a polymorphic discriminator we need ad-hoc
code for instantiating to the correct class

documentation:
Recursively get the tree node.

```
param root_node

The root node of the tree.

type root_node

dict

param parent_to_child_map

A dictionary mapping parent nodes to child nodes.

type parent_to_child_map

dict

return

The tree node.

dict
```

Schema: kgcl_schema-ontology-model
id: https://w3id.org/kgcl/ontology

description: A basic bare-bones model of an ontology or ontology-like structure. The purpose is not to provide a complete model, rather just sufficient structure for domain and range constraints in the ocl model
license: https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.ontology_model.Edge
documentation:
A relationship between two nodes.
Currently the only kinds of edges supported in KGCL:

* A subClassOf B <==> Edge(subject=A, predicate=owl:subClassOf, object=B)
* A subClassOf P some B <==> Edge(subject=A, predicate=P, object=B)
* P subPropertyOf Q <==> Edge(subject=P, predicate=owl:subPropertyOf, object=Q)

These represent the most common kind of pairwise relationship between classes, and classes are the dominant node
type in ontologies.
In future a wider variety of OWL axiom types will be supportedn through the use of an additional edge
property/slot to indicate the interpretation of the axiom, following owlstar
(https://github.com/cmungall/owlstar).
For example:
* `A subClassOf R only B <==> Edge(subject=A, predicate=P, object=B, interpretation=AllOnly)`
* `A Annotation(P,B) <==> Edge(subject=A, predicate=P, object=B, interpretation=annotationAssertion)`

Note that not all axioms are intended to map to edges. Axioms/triples where the object is a literal would be
represented as node properties. Complex OWL axioms involving nesting would have their own dedicated construct, or
may be represented generically. These are out of scope for the current version of KGCL

modules.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl
documentation: Auto generated from kgcl.yaml by pythongen.py version: 0.0.1
Generation date: 2024-11-14T14:12:04
Schema: kgcl_schema
id: https://w3id.org/kgcl
description: A data model for describing change operations at a high level on an ontology or ontology-like artefact, such as a Knowledge Graph.
* [Browse Schema](https://cmungall.github.io/knowledge-graph-change-language/)
* [GitHub](https://github.com/cmungall/knowledge-graph-change-language)
license: https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.LanguageTag
documentation: Types

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.ChangeClassType
documentation: CURIE for a class within this data model. E.g. kgcl:NodeObsoletion

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.ChangeId
documentation: Class references

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.ChangeLanguageElement
documentation: A broad grouping for all elements of the change language

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.Change
documentation: Any change perform on an ontology or knowledge graph

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.SimpleChange
documentation: A change that is about a single ontology element

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.ComplexChange
documentation: A change that is is a composition of other changes

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.MultiNodeObsoletion
documentation: A complex change consisting of multiple obsoletions.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.Transaction
documentation:
A change that is a composition of a set of changes, where those changes are treated as a single unit. Could be a
single change, or the results of an ontology diff

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.ChangeSetSummaryStatistic
documentation: A summary statistic for a set of changes of the same type, grouped by zero or more node properties

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.ChangeMixin
documentation: root class for all change mixins

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.Obsoletion
documentation: Obsoletion of an element deprecates usage of that element, but does not delete that element.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.DatatypeOrLanguageTagChange
documentation: A change in a value assertion where the value remain unchanged but either the datatype or language changes

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.AllowsAutomaticReplacementOfEdges
documentation:
Applies to an obsoletion in which annotations or edges pointing at the obsoleted node can be automatically rewired
to point to a target

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.Unobsoletion
documentation: Opposite operation of obsoletion. Rarely performed.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.Deletion
documentation: Removal of an element.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.Creation
documentation: Creation of an element.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.SubsetMembershipChange
documentation: A change in the membership status of a node with respect to a subset (view)

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.AddToSubset
documentation: placing an element inside a subset

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.RemoveFromSubset
documentation: removing an element from a subset

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.EdgeChange
documentation: A change in which the element that is the focus of the change is an edge.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.EdgeCreation
documentation: An edge change in which a de-novo edge is created. The edge is potentially annotated in the same action.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.PlaceUnder
documentation: An edge creation where the predicate is owl:subClassOf

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.EdgeDeletion
documentation: An edge change in which an edge is removed. All edge annotations/properies are removed in the same action.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.RemoveUnder
documentation: An edge deletion where the predicate is owl:subClassOf

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.EdgeObsoletion
documentation: An edge change in which an edge is obsoleted.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.EdgeRewiring
documentation: An edge change where one node is replaced with another, as in the case of obsoletion with replacement

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.MappingCreation
documentation: A specific kind of edge creation in which the created edge is a mapping.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeMove
documentation:
A node move is a combination of deleting a parent edge and adding a parent edge, where the predicate is preserved
and the object/parent node changes

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeDeepening
documentation:
A node move in which a node where the destination is a proper descendant of the original location. Note that here
descendant applied not just to subclass, but edges of any predicate in the relational graph

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeShallowing
documentation: The opposite of node deepening

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.PredicateChange
documentation: An edge change where the predicate (relationship type) is modified.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.EdgeLogicalInterpretationChange
documentation: An edge change where the subject, object, and predicate are unchanged, but the logical interpretation changes

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.LogicalAxiomChange
documentation: A simple change where a logical axiom is changed, where the logical axiom cannot be represented as an edge

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeChange
documentation: A simple change where the change is about a node

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeRename
documentation: A node change where the name (aka rdfs:label) of the node changes

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.SetLanguageForName
documentation: A node change where the string value for the name is unchanged but the language tag is set

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeAnnotationChange
documentation: A node change where the change alters node properties/annotations. TODO

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeAnnotationReplacement
documentation: A node annotation change where the change replaces a particular property value. TODO

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NewSynonym
documentation: A node synonym change where a de-novo synonym is created

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NameBecomesSynonym
documentation:
A node synonym where the name NAME of an node NODE moves to a synonym, and NODE receives a new name. This change
consists of compose of (1) a node rename where NAME is replaced by a different name (2) a new synonym

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.RemoveSynonym
documentation: A node synonym change where a synonym is deleted

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.SynonymReplacement
documentation: A node synonym change where the text of a synonym is changed

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.SynonymPredicateChange
documentation:
A node synonym change where the predicate of a synonym is changed. Background: synonyms can be represented by a
variety of predicates. For example, many OBO ontologies make use of predicates such as oio:hasExactSynonym,
oio:hasRelatedSynonym, etc

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeMappingChange
documentation: A node change where the mappings for that node are altered

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NewMapping
documentation: A node mapping change where a mapping is added to a node

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.RemoveMapping
documentation: A node mapping change where a mapping is deleted

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.MappingReplacement
documentation: A node mapping change where the object of a mapping is changed

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.MappingPredicateChange
documentation: A node mapping change where the predicate of a mapping is changed.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeMetadataAssertionChange
documentation: A node change where the metadata assertion (OWL annotations) for that node are altered

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NewMetadataAssertion
documentation: A node metadata assertion change where a metadata assertion is added to a node

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.RemoveMetadataAssertion
documentation: A node metadata assertion change where a metadata assertion is deleted

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.MetadataAssertionReplacement
documentation: A node metadata assertion change where the object of a metadata assertion is changed

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.MetadataAssertionPredicateChange
documentation: A node metadata assertion change where the predicate of a metadata assertion is changed.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeTextDefinitionChange
documentation: A node change where the text definition is changed

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NewTextDefinition
documentation: A node change where a de-novo text definition is created

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.RemoveTextDefinition
documentation: A node change where a text definition is deleted

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.TextDefinitionReplacement
documentation: A node change where a text definition is modified

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.AddNodeToSubset
documentation: Places a node inside a subset, by annotating that node

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.RemoveNodeFromSubset
documentation: Removes a node from a subset, by removing an annotation

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeObsoletion
documentation: Obsoletion of a node deprecates usage of that node, but does not delete it.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeUnobsoletion
documentation: unobsoletion of a node deprecates usage of that node. Rarely applied.

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeCreation
documentation: a node change in which a new node is created

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.ClassCreation
documentation: A node creation where the owl type is 'owl:Class'

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.ObjectPropertyCreation
documentation: A node creation where the owl type is 'ObjectProperty'

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeDeletion
documentation:
Deletion of a node from the graph. Note it is recommended nodes are obsoleted and never merged, but this operation
exists to represent deletions in ontologies, accidental or otherwise

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeDirectMerge
documentation:
An obsoletion change in which all metadata (including name/label) from the source node is deleted and added to the
target node, and edges can automatically be rewired to point to the target node

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeObsoletionWithDirectReplacement
documentation:
An obsoletion change in which information from the obsoleted node is selectively copied to a single target, and
edges can automatically be rewired to point to the target node

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.NodeObsoletionWithNoDirectReplacement
documentation: An obsoletion change in which there is no direct replacement

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.TextualDiff
documentation: A summarizing of a change on a piece of text. This could be rendered in a number of different ways

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.Configuration
documentation: The meaning of operations can be configured

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.Session
documentation: A session consists of a set of change sets bundled with the activities that generated those change sets

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.OntologyElement
documentation: Any component of an ontology or knowledge graph

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.PropertyValue
documentation: a property-value pair

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.Annotation
documentation: owl annotations. Not to be confused with annotations sensu GO

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.Node
documentation: Any named entity in an ontology. May be a class, individual, property

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.ClassNode
documentation: A node that is a class

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.InstanceNode
documentation: A node that is an individual

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.Edge
documentation:
A relationship between two nodes.
Currently the only kinds of edges supported in KGCL:

* A subClassOf B <==> Edge(subject=A, predicate=owl:subClassOf, object=B)
* A subClassOf P some B <==> Edge(subject=A, predicate=P, object=B)
* P subPropertyOf Q <==> Edge(subject=P, predicate=owl:subPropertyOf, object=Q)

These represent the most common kind of pairwise relationship between classes, and classes are the dominant node
type in ontologies.
In future a wider variety of OWL axiom types will be supportedn through the use of an additional edge
property/slot to indicate the interpretation of the axiom, following owlstar
(https://github.com/cmungall/owlstar).
For example:
* `A subClassOf R only B <==> Edge(subject=A, predicate=P, object=B, interpretation=AllOnly)`
* `A Annotation(P,B) <==> Edge(subject=A, predicate=P, object=B, interpretation=annotationAssertion)`

Note that not all axioms are intended to map to edges. Axioms/triples where the object is a literal would be
represented as node properties. Complex OWL axioms involving nesting would have their own dedicated construct, or
may be represented generically. These are out of scope for the current version of KGCL

documentation: A grouping for prov elements

documentation: a provence-generating activity

classes.name: kgcl_schema.datamodel.kgcl.Agent
documentation: a provence-generating agent
